title: ThresholdConfig
description:
Operator-tunable threshold configuration.

Top-level shape of `.config/scorecard/quality.toml`. Generates the operator-facing JSON Schema via `schemars::schema_for!` (no `cli` feature required), so a downstream `cargo build -p scorecard --no-default-features --bin emit-schema` succeeds. The JSON Schema derives drive the `quality.config.schema.json` artifact that ajv validates the committed example against.

`deny_unknown_fields` so a typo in the operator's TOML (`warn_pp_dleta` etc.) fails the parse rather than being silently dropped.

rows.description: Per-row threshold tables. Currently a single `coverage` row; new row variants add sibling fields here in lockstep with the matching `resolve_*` free function in this module.

BddFeatureSkipThresholds.description: Warn / fail thresholds for the `Row::BddFeatureLevelSkipped` variant. Both fields are unsigned integer feature-file counts; threshold semantics are inclusive on the worse side.

fail_skipped_features.description: Skipped-feature count above (or equal) which a row reports [`Status::Red`]. Typically larger than `warn_skipped_features`.

warn_skipped_features.description: Skipped-feature count above (or equal) which a row reports [`Status::Yellow`].

BddScenarioSkipThresholds.description: Warn / fail thresholds for the `Row::BddScenarioLevelSkipped` variant. Both fields are unsigned integer scenario counts; threshold semantics are inclusive on the worse side.

fail_skipped_scenarios.description: Skipped-scenario count above (or equal) which a row reports [`Status::Red`].

warn_skipped_scenarios.description: Skipped-scenario count above (or equal) which a row reports [`Status::Yellow`].

CiWallClockThresholds.description:
Warn / fail thresholds for the `Row::CiWallClockDelta` variant, expressed in seconds of total-CI-wall-clock delta vs base.

Both fields are signed: a slowdown is a positive delta, so warn / fail thresholds are themselves positive numbers. Inclusive boundary semantics: `delta_seconds == warn_seconds_delta` resolves [`Status::Yellow`]; `delta_seconds == fail_seconds_delta` resolves [`Status::Red`]. See [`resolve_ci_wall_clock`] for the full table.

fail_seconds_delta.description: Threshold above (or equal) which a row reports [`Status::Red`]. Typically positive and larger than `warn_seconds_delta`.

warn_seconds_delta.description: Threshold above (or equal) which a row reports [`Status::Yellow`]. Typically positive (slowdown).

CoverageHandlerThresholds.description:
Thresholds for the per-handler branch-coverage gate (mokumo#583).

The gate operates on entries in `Row::CoverageDelta.breakouts.by_crate[].handlers[]`. Each entry is a `(handler, branch_coverage_pct)` pair; the gate's verdict is the worst-of across handlers — one handler at 30% drives the row to Red regardless of the others.

`report_only` defaults to `true` because the producer (`coverage-breakouts`) depends on a nightly toolchain (branch coverage on rustc is nightly-only as of 1.97; see `rust-nightly-coverage-toolchain.toml`). A nightly-channel outage must not be allowed to block the merge queue — operators flip the flag to `false` in `quality.toml` once the side-channel's reliability is established. Until then the breakouts render as drill-down information without escalating row status.

fail_pct_below.description: Branch coverage % at-or-below which a handler triggers [`Status::Red`]. Default 40.0 — handlers below 40% have most negative paths uncovered and warrant a hard signal once the producer is trusted (`report_only = false`).

report_only.description: When `true` (the default), this gate's verdict is informational only — the row's status is not escalated by handler-floor breaches. The drill-down still renders. When `false`, the worst-of-handlers status feeds [`Status::worst_of`] against the underlying coverage-delta row's status.

warn_pct_below.description: Branch coverage % at-or-below which a handler triggers [`Status::Yellow`]. Operators tune via `quality.toml`. Default 60.0 — the M00 60% floor mokumo#583 was scoped against.

CoverageThresholds.description:
Warn / fail thresholds for the `Row::CoverageDelta` variant, expressed in percentage points (pp) of coverage delta vs base.

Both fields are signed: a regression is a negative delta, so warn and fail thresholds for "drops" are themselves negative numbers. Inclusive boundary semantics: `delta_pp == warn_pp_delta` resolves to [`Status::Yellow`]; `delta_pp == fail_pp_delta` resolves to [`Status::Red`]. See [`resolve_coverage_delta`] for the full table.

fail_pp_delta.description: Threshold below (or equal) which a row reports [`Status::Red`]. Typically negative and more negative than `warn_pp_delta`.

warn_pp_delta.description: Threshold below (or equal) which a row reports [`Status::Yellow`]. Typically negative.

FlakyPopulationThresholds.description:
Warn / fail thresholds for the `Row::FlakyPopulation` variant.

`marker_count` is the integer count of `// FLAKY:` markers across the operator-supplied source roots. Threshold semantics are inclusive on the worse side.

fail_marker_count.description: Marker count above (or equal) which a row reports [`Status::Red`]. Typically larger than `warn_marker_count`.

warn_marker_count.description: Marker count above (or equal) which a row reports [`Status::Yellow`].

RowsConfig.description:
Per-row threshold tables. One field per `Row` variant kind.

Adding a new row variant in `lib.rs` is paired with a new field here and a sibling `resolve_*` free function below — the four changes land together in the same commit.

coverage_handler.description: Thresholds for the per-handler branch-coverage drill-down inside `Row::CoverageDelta.breakouts.by_crate[].handlers[]` (mokumo#583). Defaults to `report_only = true` so a nightly-toolchain outage on the producer side cannot escalate the row's status — see [`CoverageHandlerThresholds`] for the field-level rationale.
